text:
Summary Abstract This IUPAC Technical Report aims to provide a thorough review and critical assessment of the analytical methodologies available for assessing the antioxidant capacity (AC) and activity of plant-derived compounds in foods, beverages, and biological samples. The ultimate goal of these measurements is to achieve accurate determination of the total antioxidant capacity (TAC) for compliance with food labeling regulations and for medical diagnostics. As a result, measuring TAC has become a preeminence goal in the field of antioxidant research in foods, beverages and biofluids, and identifying the most effective natural antioxidants and their beneficial synergies. The various assay methodologies are based either on hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) reactions—such as the oxygen radical absorption capacity (ORAC) test, the Trolox abstract radical per se (TRAP) assay or the total oxidant scavenging capacity (TOSC) assay— or on electron transfer (ET) reactions—such as ferric reducing antioxidant power (FRAP) assay, the phenolic extracts teat (TEAC), the DPPH assay, etc.

The majority of the report focuses on the CUPRAC (cupric reducing antioxidant capacity) method and its flexibility to determine both lipophilic and hydrophilic antioxidants at physiological pH as well as modified versions to determine specific reactive oxygen species and enzyme inhibition. Some obstacles, kinetic difficulties and non-comparability between different methods and techniques are also discussed with practical recommendations on possible standardisation. Many foods including fruits, vegetables, oils and cereals have been studied using the above methods before and after processing. The task group concludes that it is not possible to recommend a unique assay for determining the antioxidant activity of complex samples and therefore the determination of antioxidant activity should be based on several assays with different determining mechanisms.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
